user_message: Can you pull the context on Evergreen Solutions? I need to build the analysis.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-25 16:46 EST
Subject: Follow-up: deployment blockers Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with deployment blockers. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Okafor <samantha.okafor@evergreensol.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 09:03 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Evergreen Solutions

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about deployment blockers. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Evergreen Solutions
Date: 2024-03-20
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions), David Chen (Microsoft), David (Evergreen Solutions)

[00:02:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:08:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:15:00] David (Evergreen Solutions): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:20:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:26:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:30:00] Samantha Okafor (Evergreen Solutions): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:33:00] David (Evergreen Solutions): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-B trap (Exposes raw email/chat content in customer-facing language); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-B failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-B failure mode (Exposes raw email/chat content in customer-facing language)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-B -- Exposes raw email/chat content in customer-facing language
